manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.
Your opponent's team words are: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
The kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

TARGETS: line, string, post, spine, train, missile

    *   Distractor Words: greece, strike, satellite.
    *   Kill Words: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.
    *   Goal: Connect as many team words as possible without touching opponent, distractor, or kill words.

2.  **Evaluate Team Words:**
    *   spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.

    *   Group 2: Space/Sky? moon, missile, satellite (distractor), ground.
    *   Group 3: Transportation? train, missile (projectile), sub (opponent).
    *   Group 4: Abstract? luck, line, string.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.
Your opponent's team words are: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
The kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

CLUE: SEQUENCE
TARGETS: line, string, post, spine, train, missile

The words string, line, train were guessed correctly. The word sub was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, post.
Remaining words for your opponent are: stadium, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Remaining distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
Remaining kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.
Your opponent's team words are: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
The kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

CLUE: SEQUENCE
TARGETS: line, string, post, spine, train, missile

The words string, line, train were guessed correctly. The word sub was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, post.
Remaining words for your opponent are: stadium, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Remaining distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
Remaining kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

CLUE: POLE
TARGETS: spine, post, moon, ground

The words post, ground were guessed correctly. The word strike was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Goal: Provide a single-word clue and a list of target words from my team's remaining words.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Constraints:
        *   Single-word clue.
        *   Clue must be semantically related to target words.
        *   Clue cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.
        *   Must be short, no extra text.
        *   Context: Codenames game simulation.
    *   Current State:
        *   My Team Words Remaining: spine, missile, luck, moon.
        *   Opponent Words: stadium, dinosaur, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
        *   Distractor Words: greece, satellite.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.
Your opponent's team words are: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
The kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

CLUE: SEQUENCE
TARGETS: line, string, post, spine, train, missile

The words string, line, train were guessed correctly. The word sub was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, post.
Remaining words for your opponent are: stadium, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Remaining distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
Remaining kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

CLUE: POLE
TARGETS: spine, post, moon, ground

The words post, ground were guessed correctly. The word strike was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: spine, missile, luck, moon.
Remaining words for your opponent are: stadium, dinosaur, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Remaining distractor words are: greece, satellite.
Remaining kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

CLUE: PHASE
TARGETS: moon, luck

The word moon was guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '.
